# Cold Handoff — `openclown-assistant` Orchestrator → New Devin Account

**Generated:** 2026-05-02 ~03:50 UTC, auto-cold after the close of the **TKT-011** (Summary Recommendation Scheduler) cycle — the **second Ticket Orchestrator (TO) delegation pilot** — plus four structural lessons captured in BACKLOG-008 and one CONTRIBUTING.md clarification (PR #77).
**Type:** COLD (auto-generated, formal state only — no texture / no observations).
**Handoff target:** Fresh Devin session in any account that can be authenticated to the `OpenClown-bot/openclown-assistant` repo.
**Outgoing session:** `https://app.devin.ai/sessions/c08cdbbfbcda4838837ad27d86f9271d` (this is the part-2 snapshot that follows `2026-05-01-session-4.md`; same Devin Orchestrator account / same long-running session continued through TKT-011 dispatch → ratification → closure-PR → TKT-013 dispatch prep).

---

## 0. What you (new Devin) are looking at

You are taking over the **AI-engineering mentor / orchestrator** role for `openclown-assistant`. This file is your full bootstrap. It assumes you have **not** read `2026-05-01-session-4.md` (its TKT-010 retrospective is still accurate) but you should read it for full context after this file's self-checks.

Your three core obligations (full prompt in `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md`, read it after the self-checks in §1):

1. **Teach** the PO (non-engineer founder learning the SDLC).
2. **Push back** with evidence; cite artifacts and ADRs. Sycophancy is a failure mode.
3. **Protect docs-as-code:** every architectural decision must live in a versioned markdown file in git. "We agreed in chat" is not a decision.

You are **NOT** one of the four pipeline LLM agents (Business Planner, Architect, Executor, Reviewer) and you are **NOT** the per-TKT Ticket Orchestrator. You are the strategic / cross-TKT / cross-reviewer-audit ratifier.

**Session-4 part-2 deltas you must absorb (full detail in §6 below):**

1. **Second TO pilot ran on TKT-011 and closed merge-safe.** Kimi `pass` on iter-2 (commit `f195017`); 6 substantive Executor commits followed iter-3..iter-8 driven by PR-Agent + Devin Review findings; PR-Agent persistent review settled to final HEAD `4e3e818` with `⚡ no major issues + 🔒 no security`. Devin ratification audit (pass-2) treated the iter-3..8 procedural gap as substance-clean / contract-broken and the stuck PR-Agent CI workflow as infrastructure failure under DO authority per session-4 §6.6 precedent.
2. **CONTRIBUTING.md MAY-write column for the Devin Orchestrator clarified** in closure-PR #77 (PO authorised "этот вопрос реши сам, закрой его полностью" verbatim in the PR body): closure-PRs may now span TKT / RV / BACKLOG bodies after Reviewer verdict, including (a) §10 Execution Log post-Executor closure entries, (b) RV frontmatter promotion to `status: approved` with `approved_at` / `approved_after_iters` / `approved_by` / `approved_note`, (c) clerical RV file rename when the Reviewer-chosen numbering violates the `TKT-N ↔ RV-CODE-N` convention, (d) duplicate-§10 boilerplate removal when the empty TEMPLATE.md H2 collides with the Executor-filled §10 H2, (e) `assigned_executor` post-hoc to match actual run (formality fix). This codifies four years of clerical practice that previously lived only in PR-by-PR PO authorisation.
3. **Four structural TKT-NEW lessons captured in BACKLOG-008** (`docs/backlog/summary-followups.md`, new file): (i) launcher-asserts-frontmatter-executor (TKT-011 ran on glm-5.1 while frontmatter said qwen-3.6-plus — silent deviation), (ii) reviewer-reengagement-after-substantive-pushes (iter-3..8 procedural gap), (iii) pr-agent-tail-latency (now 2 of 2 final-HEAD runs across 2 TO pilots — TKT-010 22-min outlier, TKT-011 stuck/cancelled), (iv) reviewer-rv-code-numbering-convention (Kimi mis-numbered RV-CODE-016 instead of RV-CODE-011; DO renamed in closure-PR). Plus 2 PR-Agent informational carry-overs (F-PA-1 loader-context-error-message, F-PA-2 loader-async-fs).
4. **TKT-013 (Deployment Packaging) dispatched as third TO pilot.** PR #78 (clerical: TKT-013 ArchSpec refresh @0.2.0 → @0.4.0, same pattern as PR #74 for TKT-011) merged 2026-05-02. Bootstrap message handed to PO for opencode dispatch on glm-5.1.
5. **AGENTS.md note (2026-04-30):** Devin Review is deprecated as a supplementary reviewer due to ACU exhaustion; in practice it still posts informational comments on some PRs but its verdict is no longer required for merge. Treat any Devin Review findings as informational unless they describe a verified bug.

## 4. Pipeline (stable; one note)

Five roles unchanged. PR-Agent (Qwen 3.6 Plus through OmniRoute) is the supplementary reviewer alongside Kimi K2.6. Devin Review is deprecated per AGENTS.md as of 2026-04-30 (ACU exhaustion); in practice it still posts inline comments occasionally, but its verdict is no longer required for merge. The five roles and their default models / runtimes match `AGENTS.md` exactly — re-read it if anything below is unclear.

The **Devin Orchestrator** (you) is the strategic / cross-TKT / ratification layer above the per-TKT TO. See `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md` §11 for the TO ↔ Devin contract.

## 5. Roles and write-zones

Canonical: `CONTRIBUTING.md` § Roles. PR #77 expanded the Devin Orchestrator MAY-write column — re-read that row in full.

**Devin Orchestrator (you) write-zone (post-PR #77):** `docs/session-log/`, `docs/backlog/` (light edits / new entries), ticket frontmatter promotions (`status`, `arch_ref`, `version`, `updated`, `assigned_executor` post-hoc to match actual run, `completed_at`, `completed_by`, `completed_note`) + light reference-pinning in ticket body during promotion + closure-PRs spanning TKT / RV / BACKLOG bodies after Reviewer verdict (including §10 Execution Log post-Executor closure entries; RV frontmatter promotion to `status: approved` with `approved_at` / `approved_after_iters` / `approved_by` / `approved_note`; clerical RV file rename when the original Reviewer-chosen numbering violates the TKT-N ↔ RV-CODE-N convention; duplicate-§10 boilerplate removal when the empty template H2 collides with the Executor-filled §10 H2) + cross-TKT shared-interface conflict resolution + ratification audit + final merge-safe sign-off. Edits to `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `docs/prompts/`, `docs/meta/`, `src/`, `tests/`, formal artifact bodies (PRD/ARCH/ADR/RV) require explicit PO authorisation recorded verbatim in the PR body.

### 6.4 TKT-011 second-TO-pilot retrospective

The TO bootstrap pattern (per `docs/prompts/ticket-orchestrator.md` § Bootstrap input + `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md` §11.2) ran end-to-end on TKT-011 with one formal Reviewer iter and six post-Reviewer-pass Executor commits driven by PR-Agent + Devin Review findings:

| Iter | Executor verdict | Reviewer verdict | PR-Agent state | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| iter-1 | GLM pushed PR #75 head (initial) | Kimi `pass_with_changes` (F-M1 timezone, F-L1 dead code, F-L2 weak assertion) | settled clean | TO classified findings substantive; iter-2 fix dispatch |
| iter-2 | GLM pushed `f195017` | Kimi `pass` (all prior findings resolved) | settled clean | **Final formal Reviewer verdict.** Kimi was not re-engaged after this point. |
| iter-3..iter-8 | GLM pushed 6 substantive commits driven by PR-Agent inline findings + 1 Devin Review finding (NFKC normalisation, zero-width strip, `<persona>` delimiters, homoglyph normalisation, persona cache path, monthly-target compute fix, persona-text escape against `</persona>` breakout) | (none — Kimi not re-engaged) | persistent review settled to final HEAD `4e3e818` with `⚡ no major issues + 🔒 no security`; workflow run stuck IN_PROGRESS on rerun | DO ratification audit treated this as substance-clean / contract-broken: PR-Agent + Devin Review covered substance, but the cross-reviewer audit Hard rule "Reviewer pass + post-pass substantive pushes require Reviewer iter-N+1" was technically violated. |

**Cross-reviewer audit pass-1 (TO):** all findings classified RESOLVED in current HEAD. iter-3..8 commits flagged as procedural-gap-but-substance-covered.
**Cross-reviewer audit pass-2 (Devin ratification):** independent re-classification confirmed substance clean; PR-Agent CI workflow stuck status treated as infra failure under DO authority per session-4 §6.6 precedent (TKT-010 22-min outlier — now 2 of 2 final-HEAD runs across both pilots show tail-latency / stuck pattern).

**Anomalies fixed in closure-PR #77:**
- TKT-011 frontmatter `assigned_executor: "qwen-3.6-plus" → "glm-5.1"` (PO-confirmed formality fix: actual run was on glm-5.1).
- RV file renamed `RV-CODE-016-pr-75-tkt-011.md → RV-CODE-011-tkt-011-summary-recommendation-scheduler.md` + frontmatter `id: RV-CODE-016 → RV-CODE-011` (Kimi mis-numbered as next-sequential `016` instead of TKT-aligned `011`).
- Duplicate `## 10. Execution Log` H2 (template boilerplate + Executor-filled table both used the same heading) collapsed to single H2 + restored unnumbered `## Handoff Checklist` per TEMPLATE.md convention (initial Devin Review F1 fix in closure-PR was wrong shape — renumbered to `## 11.` placed before §10; corrected in closure-PR commit `439445b`).

### 6.5 Closure-PR pattern (stable across PR #73 + PR #77)

Once Executor PR + Reviewer PR are merged, open a closure-PR with:

1. TKT body:
   - Frontmatter: `status: in_review → done`; new `completed_at` / `completed_by` / `completed_note`.
   - §10 Execution Log: append closure entries (Executor final iter, Reviewer verdict, PR-Agent settle / stuck status, TO pass-1 audit, Devin pass-2 ratification, PO merge, closure-PR opening).
2. RV body:
   - Frontmatter: `status: in_review → approved`; new `approved_at` / `approved_after_iters` / `approved_by` / `approved_note`.
   - File rename if the Reviewer-chosen numbering violates the `TKT-N ↔ RV-CODE-N` convention.
3. BACKLOG file:
   - One new file per TKT for that TKT's deferred follow-ups + structural lessons learned.
4. (When applicable) `CONTRIBUTING.md` MAY-write column clarifications, with PO verbatim authorisation cited in the PR body.

Validator: must remain `validated N artifact(s); 0 failed` throughout. Verify before opening the PR.

### 6.6 Pipeline-integrity improvements from second pilot (BACKLOG-008)

Four structural lessons captured in `docs/backlog/summary-followups.md` (BACKLOG-008). Each is a TKT-NEW candidate; until they are implemented as formal tickets, the Devin Orchestrator and Ticket Orchestrator must enforce them as runtime constraints on every pilot:

1. **launcher-asserts-frontmatter-executor.** opencode launcher must read the TKT frontmatter `assigned_executor` and either auto-select that model in OmniRoute or fail-closed if PO-selected model differs. TKT-011 ran on glm-5.1 while frontmatter said qwen-3.6-plus — silent deviation discovered only post-hoc. Until implemented: Devin Orchestrator must verify the actual Executor model against frontmatter before drafting Iter-1 NUDGE; if a deviation is unavoidable, document the override + bump the frontmatter post-hoc in the closure-PR with explicit `completed_note` disclosure.

2. **reviewer-reengagement-after-substantive-pushes.** Codify in `docs/prompts/ticket-orchestrator.md` (currently NOT codified) that any substantive Executor commit pushed after Reviewer verdict `pass` must trigger a fresh Reviewer iteration before TO drafts hand-back. Pure clerical commits (typo fixes in comments, formatting, lockfile regeneration) are exempt; when in doubt, re-engage Kimi. The TKT-011 pilot accumulated 6 substantive Executor commits after Kimi iter-2 pass without re-review (PR-Agent + Devin Review covered substance, but the contract was technically broken). Until codified: TO must apply this rule manually on every cycle.

3. **pr-agent-tail-latency.** Investigate OmniRoute / Fireworks throughput on PR-Agent's prompt shape. 2 of 2 final-HEAD runs across 2 TO pilots show tail-latency / stuck patterns: TKT-010 had a 22-min outlier (normal is 3–9 min); TKT-011 final-HEAD run ended CANCELLED, rerun stuck IN_PROGRESS until Devin Orchestrator overrode under DO authority. The PR #71 12-min hard-timeout mitigates impact, but root cause is not in our orbit (likely OmniRoute throughput on long prompts). Pattern is structural, not random. Until investigated: Devin Orchestrator may treat stuck PR-Agent CI status as infrastructure failure when the persistent review has settled to final HEAD with clean verdict, per session-4 §6.6 precedent — this is an explicit DO-authority override of the formal `conclusion` check.

4. **reviewer-rv-code-numbering-convention.** Add an explicit rule to `docs/prompts/reviewer.md` (currently NOT codified) that Kimi must use `RV-CODE-N` to match `TKT-N`, not next-sequential numbering. Add a CI check in `scripts/validate_docs.py` that enforces `target_pr` ↔ `id` mapping (the file's `id` must match the TKT id from the `ticket_ref`). Kimi mis-numbered TKT-011's review as `RV-CODE-016` (after `RV-CODE-015` for TKT-015) instead of `RV-CODE-011`; DO fixed it in closure-PR via `git mv` + frontmatter `id` flip. Until codified: TO must explicitly require `RV-CODE-NNN` in the Reviewer NUDGE matching the TKT id.

Plus two PR-Agent informational carry-overs (low severity, defer-to-BACKLOG was the audit verdict): F-PA-1 loader-context-error-message (differentiate `loadPersona` startup vs runtime error context); F-PA-2 loader-async-fs (convert `readFileSync` to async `fs.promises.readFile` with single-flight cache).

### 6.7 TKT-013 third-TO-pilot retrospective

The TO bootstrap pattern (per `docs/prompts/ticket-orchestrator.md` § Bootstrap input + `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md` §11.2) ran end-to-end on TKT-013 on 2026-05-02 with FOUR formal Reviewer iterations, each matching a substantive Executor push — a major procedural improvement vs TKT-011's single-iter-pass + 6 post-pass commits gap.

| Iter | Executor verdict | Reviewer verdict | PR-Agent state | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| iter-1 | GLM pushed PR #80 head `5cacba8` | Kimi `fail` (F-H1 rollback unreachable, F-H2 missing shell tests, F-M1 unused volume, F-M2 wrong service names) | settled clean | TO classified all findings substantive; iter-2 fix dispatch |
| iter-2 | GLM pushed `70efee1` | Kimi `fail` (prior 4 RESOLVED; new F-M3 Dockerfile healthcheck invalid + 3 new lows) | settled clean | TO classified F-M3 substantive; iter-3 fix dispatch |
| iter-3 | GLM pushed `5d97acc4` | Kimi `pass_with_changes` (F-M3 RESOLVED; F-L3, F-L4 remaining as low) | settled clean | TO ran cross-reviewer audit; PR-Agent surfaced wildcard metrics-bind; iter-4 fix dispatch |
| iter-4 | GLM pushed `b50443e5` | Kimi `pass_with_changes` (cross-reviewer wildcard concern RESOLVED via `METRICS_HOST=metrics`; F-L3, F-L4, F-L6 remain as low) | persistent review settled to final HEAD `b50443e5` with `⚡ no major issues + 🔒 no security`; workflow run cancelled at 12m12s (PR #71 hard-timeout) | **Final formal Reviewer verdict.** Zero post-pass substantive Executor commits. |

**Cross-reviewer audit pass-1 (TO):** all High (F-H1, F-H2) and all Medium (F-M1, F-M2, F-M3) findings RESOLVED in current HEAD; 3 Low (F-L3, F-L4, F-L6) deferred-with-rationale to BACKLOG-009; F-L5 classified no-action (correct Docker network detail). PR-Agent informational items independently classified — 3 RESOLVED in iter-4/5, 3 matched Kimi's Low classification, 1 not-a-finding (`infra/omniroute/README.md` is in TKT-013 §5 Outputs but outside `validate_docs.py` artifact scope). Cross-reviewer audit pass-2 (Devin ratification) independently re-verified the same.

**PR-Agent CI tail-latency — 3-of-3 across 3 pilots:** TKT-010 final-HEAD = 22-min outlier (normal 3–9). TKT-011 final-HEAD = stuck IN_PROGRESS → cancelled. TKT-013 final-HEAD on BOTH Executor PR #80 + Reviewer PR #81 = cancelled at 12m12s. Pattern is structural, not random. BACKLOG-009 §TKT-NEW-pr-agent-ci-tail-latency-investigation-CRITICAL escalates the lesson from BACKLOG-008's Medium-High to **Critical**: the DO override is currently load-bearing for every closure (should be fallback, not steady-state). Concrete short-/medium-/long-term investigation paths captured in BACKLOG-009.

**Operational anomaly:** PO reported both Executor and Reviewer opencode sessions interrupted mid-iter (TUI "continue" prompt at 49% token budget — not exhaustion; cause unclear, possibly Fireworks API hiccup or OmniRoute auth blip). The cycle survived because TO recovered ad-hoc, but there is no codified resume protocol. BACKLOG-009 §TKT-NEW-opencode-session-interrupt-resilience captures the lesson with a 5-step ITER-N RESUME protocol (git status / log / pending tool calls / explicit committed-vs-in-flight classification / resume-or-rewind decision) for future pilots.

**Procedural improvement vs TKT-011:** Reviewer was re-engaged on every substantive Executor push (iter-1→iter-4). BACKLOG-008 §reviewer-reengagement-after-substantive-pushes constraint was enforced by TO via explicit reminder in iter-N+1 dispatch NUDGE. **The constraint is portable** and worked exactly as intended on this pilot.

**RV-CODE-013 numbering correct, file naming residual:** Reviewer used the correct id `RV-CODE-013` per BACKLOG-008 §reviewer-rv-code-numbering-convention (TO enforced via explicit Reviewer NUDGE language). Filename was `RV-CODE-013-pr-80-tkt-013.md` instead of canonical `RV-CODE-013-tkt-013-deployment-packaging.md` — corrected in closure-PR #82 via `git mv`. BACKLOG-009 §TKT-NEW-rv-code-file-naming-canonical codifies the canonical pattern for the Reviewer NUDGE template.

### 6.8 Pipeline-integrity improvements from third pilot (BACKLOG-009)

Seven entries captured in `docs/backlog/deployment-followups.md` (BACKLOG-009): three F-L carry-overs (Kimi iter-4 deferred-low findings) + four structural TKT-NEW lessons. The structural set, all of which constrain Devin / Ticket Orchestrator behavior on TKT-012 and TKT-014:

1. **opencode-session-interrupt-resilience** (Medium). Codify ITER-N RESUME protocol after a 49%-token-budget Executor + Reviewer interruption pattern surfaced on TKT-013. TO must NOT silently resume; must request explicit committed-vs-in-flight classification before authorising further work. Should be implemented before pilot 5 (TKT-014) lands.

2. **pr-agent-ci-tail-latency-investigation-CRITICAL** (escalated from BACKLOG-008 Medium-High to Critical). 3-of-3 final-HEAD runs across 3 pilots stalled. PR #71 hard-timeout mitigates impact but DO override is load-bearing for every closure. Three investigation tracks: per-PR PR-Agent timing breakdown in next 1–2 pilots; controlled experiment with synthetic PR; long-term Q-INFRA against OmniRoute / Qwen routing layer (consider PR-Agent reviewer model swap to GLM 5.1 to test whether tail-latency is Qwen-specific).

3. **agents-md-vs-llm-routing-md-runtime-mismatch** (Low, surfaced during 4th-pilot prep). AGENTS.md row "Code Executor" lists runtime `opencode + OmniRoute` for ALL three Executor models; `docs/knowledge/llm-routing.md` row "Executor (specialist) Codex GPT-5.5" lists runtime `Codex CLI`. The two sources disagree. Empirical resolution by PO during TKT-012 iter-1 dispatch will fix the stale doc; closure-PR will update whichever artifact is wrong.

4. **rv-code-file-naming-canonical** (Low). Reinforce in `docs/prompts/reviewer.md` + Reviewer NUDGE template the canonical filename pattern `RV-CODE-NNN-tkt-NNN-{title-slug}.md`. The Reviewer artifact id is already enforced via BACKLOG-008 §reviewer-rv-code-numbering-convention; this entry extends to the file name. Optionally augment `scripts/validate_docs.py` to warn (not fail) on legacy `pr-NN` segments to auto-surface drift.

F-L follow-ups (deferred to a future deployment-area Polish-pass ticket; can be batched since all three touch the same files): F-L3 cleaner JSON parse errors in `migrate-vps-kbju.sh`; F-L4 rollback success-path test in `tests/deployment/scripts.test.ts`; F-L6 `server.on('error')` handler in `src/deployment/healthCheck.ts`.

### 6.9 TKT-012 fourth-TO-pilot retrospective (first non-GLM Executor)

The TO bootstrap pattern ran end-to-end on TKT-012 on 2026-05-02 with TWO substantive Executor iterations and THREE Reviewer iterations. The Reviewer count exceeds Executor count because Kimi performed an extra iter-3 dedicated to verifying PR-Agent's final-head focus areas (a procedural improvement codified into TO Reviewer NUDGE for this pilot).

| Iter | Executor verdict | Reviewer verdict | PR-Agent state | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| iter-1 | Codex GPT-5.5 pushed `ae840d2` (substantive impl) + `809ecb3` (clerical status flip) | Kimi `fail` (F-H1 deletion-order FK violation `meal_drafts` before `confirmed_meals`, F-M1 negated-phrase false-positive, F-L1 weak deletion-order test, F-L2 weak `AUDIT_DB_URL` lint scan) | settled to `ae840d2` clean | TO classified F-H1 + F-M1 + F-L1 substantive; iter-2 fix dispatch with explicit re-engagement reminder per BACKLOG-008 §reviewer-reengagement-after-substantive-pushes |
| iter-2 | Codex pushed `24e0c42` (substantive fix) | Kimi iter-2 (verify-fixes): F-H1 + F-M1 + F-L1 RESOLVED; F-L2 still DEFERRED | settled to `24e0c42` with verdict `⚡ no major issues + 🔒 no security concerns + 🧪 PR contains tests`; 2 focus areas surfaced (F-PA-1 Privacy Compliance Risk, F-PA-2 Flaky Concurrency Test) | TO scheduled iter-3 dedicated to PR-Agent final-head finding classification |
| iter-3 | (none — same HEAD `24e0c42`) | Kimi `pass` (F-PA-1 NOT A FINDING — PRD-001@0.2.0 US-8 establishes `/forget_me` as canonical; mixed-intent ambiguity correctly errs toward caution; F-PA-2 REAL/LOW deferred — production serialization is `pg_advisory_xact_lock`, unit test only verifies service-level call ordering) | persistent review remained settled to `24e0c42`; workflow run id 25251565864 cancelled @12m11s (PR #71 hard-timeout) | **Final formal Reviewer verdict.** Zero post-pass substantive Executor commits. |

**Cross-reviewer audit pass-1 (TO):** all 4 Kimi findings classified (3 RESOLVED, 1 DEFERRED with rationale that fix lies outside TKT-012@0.1.0 §5 outputs); both PR-Agent focus areas independently classified (1 NOT A FINDING, 1 DEFERRED with rationale that production correctness is unaffected). Cross-reviewer audit pass-2 (Devin ratification) independently re-verified all 6 classifications and matched TO at 100%.

**PR-Agent CI tail-latency — 4-of-4 across 4 pilots:** TKT-010 final-HEAD = 22-min outlier. TKT-011 final-HEAD = stuck IN_PROGRESS → cancelled. TKT-013 final-HEAD on BOTH PRs = cancelled @12m12s. TKT-012 Executor PR #84 final-HEAD = cancelled @12m11s; **TKT-012 Reviewer PR #85 final-HEAD = SUCCESS (small doc-only diff, no tail-latency)**. Pattern persists structurally; the success on Reviewer PR #85 confirms the tail-latency is correlated with code-PR diff size or substance, not just any PR-Agent invocation. BACKLOG-009 `§pr-agent-ci-tail-latency-investigation-CRITICAL` rule applied: workflow stall classified as expected CI infra failure under DO authority because persistent review settled to final HEAD with clean substantive verdict.

**Empirical runtime resolution:** PO ran `opencode + Codex GPT-5.5 high` via OmniRoute successfully on the PO's Windows PC; Executor PR #84 body recorded observed runtime as `cx/gpt-5.5-high / omniroute/cx/gpt-5.5-high`. This empirically resolved BACKLOG-009 `§agents-md-vs-llm-routing-md-runtime-mismatch` in favour of AGENTS.md (canonical runtime `opencode + OmniRoute` for ALL three Executor model variants). `docs/knowledge/llm-routing.md` rows 40 (Executor specialist) + 42 (Reviewer auto, also stale per AGENTS.md 2026-04-30 Devin Review deprecation) reconciled in closure-PR #86 with explicit PO authorisation recorded verbatim in PR body per CONTRIBUTING.md row 23 protocol (since `docs/knowledge/` is outside the standard Devin Orchestrator write-zone).

**Procedural improvement vs prior pilots:** Both BACKLOG-008/009 portable constraints — `§reviewer-reengagement-after-substantive-pushes` AND `§reviewer-rv-code-numbering-convention` + `§rv-code-file-naming-canonical` — were enforced from iter-1 of TO Reviewer NUDGE. RV-CODE-012 frontmatter `id: RV-CODE-012` and file path `docs/reviews/RV-CODE-012-tkt-012-right-to-delete-audit.md` were both correct without any post-hoc rename. **First pilot to enforce id + filename simultaneously without closure-PR cleanup** (TKT-009/010/011/013 all required at least one rename). The lessons from BACKLOG-008/009 are now fully portable into the dispatch protocol.

### 6.10 Pipeline-integrity improvements from fourth pilot (BACKLOG-010)

Three entries captured in `docs/backlog/right-to-delete-followups.md` (BACKLOG-010): two F-L carry-overs (Kimi F-L2 + PR-Agent F-PA-2 deferred-low findings) + one resolution entry closing a prior BACKLOG-009 entry empirically.

1. **TKT-NEW-audit-db-url-repo-wide-lint** (Low; F-L2 carry-over). Replace the sampled-file scan in `tests/privacy/tenantAudit.test.ts` with a repo-wide enforcement: either an ESLint `no-restricted-syntax` rule against `process.env.AUDIT_DB_URL` outside `src/privacy/tenantAudit.ts`, or a CI grep script that asserts the only match is the audit runner. Defer to a future hygiene-pass ticket; can batch with other repo-wide lint hardening tickets.

2. **TKT-NEW-right-to-delete-concurrency-test-barrier** (Low; F-PA-2 carry-over). Replace the `waitForCall` microtask polling loop in `tests/privacy/rightToDelete.test.ts` with `vi.useFakeTimers()` + `vi.runAllTimersAsync()`, or with an explicit Promise-based `releaseConfirmMeal` hook on the mock repository. Production correctness is unaffected (real serialization is PostgreSQL-native `pg_advisory_xact_lock`); this is purely a test-determinism fix. Defer; can batch with `audit-db-url-repo-wide-lint`.

3. **TKT-NEW-runtime-mismatch-empirically-resolved-codex-via-omniroute** (Low; resolution entry, RESOLVED in this closure-PR). Empirical confirmation that `opencode + Codex GPT-5.5 high → OmniRoute → OpenAI` is reachable end-to-end. Closes BACKLOG-009 `§agents-md-vs-llm-routing-md-runtime-mismatch` in favour of AGENTS.md as the canonical runtime source; `docs/knowledge/llm-routing.md` rows 40 + 42 reconciled in closure-PR #86. Entry retained in BACKLOG-010 for audit-trail completeness only.

### 6.11 Texture (carry from session-4 §6.7 + extension)

The cross-reviewer audit Hard rule (PR #67), the iter-N continuation rule (PR #71), and the PR-Agent settle-on-final-HEAD requirement (PR #71) were the parents of the TKT-011 second-pilot constraints. The TKT-011 retrospective's iter-3..8 procedural gap is the natural successor to F-PA-17 (HTML-escape miss caught only by PO push-back, recorded in `2026-05-01-session-3.md` §6.7) and the TKT-010 PR-Agent tail-latency outlier — each successive pilot exposes one more structural gap that becomes a new invariant. The pattern continues to validate the two-phase audit model (TO pass-1 + DO pass-2) introduced after F-PA-17.

The TKT-013 third pilot continued this pattern at a higher signal-to-noise ratio: BACKLOG-008's `§reviewer-reengagement-after-substantive-pushes` constraint (lessons from TKT-011's iter-3..8 gap) was enforced perfectly on TKT-013 with zero post-pass substantive commits and 4-of-4 Reviewer iters matching substantive Executor pushes — **the lesson is portable** and the codified rule worked as designed. BACKLOG-008's `§reviewer-rv-code-numbering-convention` constraint also held (Reviewer used `RV-CODE-013` correctly; only the residual filename pattern needed cleanup). The two structural gaps that this pilot newly surfaced are operational, not procedural: opencode session interrupts at non-exhausted token budgets (BACKLOG-009 §opencode-session-interrupt-resilience), and the now-3-of-3 PR-Agent tail-latency pattern that escalates from tolerable-anomaly to load-bearing-override (BACKLOG-009 §pr-agent-ci-tail-latency-investigation-CRITICAL). The fourth pilot (TKT-012) was the first non-GLM Executor cycle and surfaced a fourth gap — the AGENTS.md vs llm-routing.md runtime mismatch (BACKLOG-009 §agents-md-vs-llm-routing-md-runtime-mismatch) — which was empirically resolved during the cycle (PO ran `opencode + Codex GPT-5.5 high` via OmniRoute successfully; `docs/knowledge/llm-routing.md` rows 40 + 42 reconciled in closure-PR #86 with explicit PO authorisation per CONTRIBUTING.md row 23 protocol). Pattern persists: each pilot exposes one or more structural gaps that become new invariants for the next.

The TKT-012 fourth pilot also delivered the first **executor-uncorrelation calibration data** since the pipeline began (Codex GPT-5.5 ran end-to-end without runtime-specific issues; 2 Executor iterations + 3 Reviewer iterations; F-H1 + F-M1 + F-L1 all RESOLVED in iter-2; iter-3 cleanly verified PR-Agent's final-head focus areas and reached `pass`). **Two BACKLOG-008/009 constraints — reviewer re-engagement AND RV-CODE numbering+canonical filename — were both enforced from the first iteration of this pilot's TO Reviewer NUDGE**, with zero post-hoc cleanup needed in closure-PR #86. This is the first pilot where ALL prior pilot lessons were operationally portable into the dispatch protocol; TKT-014 inherits the full constraint set (BACKLOG-008 + BACKLOG-009 + BACKLOG-010, 9 structural TKT-NEW + 1 resolution entry) cleanly. The fifth pilot (TKT-014) will be the second non-GLM Executor cycle on Qwen 3.6 Plus, completing the 3-family calibration matrix and providing one final natural experiment for BACKLOG-009 `§pr-agent-ci-tail-latency-investigation-CRITICAL` (because PR-Agent also runs Qwen 3.6 Plus, correlated tail-latency on Executor would be diagnostic evidence supporting investigation track #3, the Qwen-routing-layer hypothesis).

---

## 7. After self-checks pass + required reading done

Reply to the PO with:

1. **5-line state summary** in your own words (proves you read this file, the current ArchSpec, open Tickets, BACKLOG-008's four structural lessons, and `2026-05-01-session-4.md`'s TKT-010 retrospective).
2. **Role-confirmation:** "I am the Devin Orchestrator. Per session-3 + session-4 + this session-5, my responsibilities include cross-TKT coordination, ratification audit on TO hand-backs (now including PR-Agent settle-on-final-HEAD verification per PR #71 and the iter-N+ Reviewer-re-engagement constraint per BACKLOG-008), and final merge-safe sign-off. I will not write code, will not edit PRDs / ArchSpecs / ADRs / role prompts, and will only modify `docs/session-log/`, `docs/backlog/` (lightly), and ticket / review frontmatter promotion fields per the PR #77 expansion. Other CONTRIBUTING.md / AGENTS.md / `docs/prompts/` / `docs/meta/` edits require explicit PO authorisation recorded verbatim in the PR body."
3. **One concrete proposed next action** with reasoning. Default proposal sequence depends on whether the TKT-013 pilot is mid-flight or already closed:
   - If TKT-013 PRs (Executor + Reviewer) are open and the TO has handed back closure-ready: run the **ratification audit pass-2** (independent re-classification of all Reviewer + PR-Agent findings to RESOLVED / deferred-with-rationale; verify PR-Agent settle-on-final-HEAD; check for substantive post-Reviewer-pass commits and require Reviewer iter-N+1 if present per BACKLOG-008 §reviewer-reengagement). Then sign off merge-safe and prep the closure-PR after PO merges.
   - If TKT-013 PRs are not yet open: confirm with the PO that the third TO pilot is mid-flight on opencode (glm-5.1) and hold pattern (no Devin action required until TO hand-back).
   - If TKT-013 already closed: select the **next TKT** for the fourth pilot. Default candidate: **TKT-012 Right To Delete Audit** if `docs/prompts/ticket-orchestrator.md` has been updated to enforce the Reviewer re-engagement rule and / or the launcher mismatch fix has been implemented (BACKLOG-008 §TKT-NEW-launcher-asserts-frontmatter-executor); otherwise the next ready TKT with frontmatter `assigned_executor: glm-5.1` to keep launcher-mismatch risk at zero.
